name: Red Snapper Veracruz Style
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Olive Oil or Vegetable Oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
2. Season fish fillets with salt and pepper.
3. Dust the fish with flour and coat evenly.
4. Heat oil in a baking dish over medium-high heat until hot but not smoking.
5. Place seasoned fish fillets in the center of the hot oil.
6. Sear each side for about 1-2 minutes, or until golden brown.
7. Remove fish from the pan and set aside on a plate.
8. In the same pan, add remaining oil if needed and sauté onion and garlic over medium heat until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
9. Add tomato puree, pimento stuffed olives, water, lime juice, pickled jalapeno pepper, sugar, cinnamon, cloves, and bay leaf to the pan.
10. Stir to combine all ingredients.
11. Return fish fillets to the pan with the sauce.
12. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing flavors to meld.
13. Remove from heat and let rest for a few minutes before serving.
